div.div: · double glass bulb containing red and white pellets

The glass object has two connected bulbs with small pellets of two colors mixed together. Your goal: sort all pellets of one color into one bulb and all of the other color into the other bulb — without opening the glass. Tilt the object to consolidate all pellets in one bulb. Shake gently — slight differences in size or weight cause the pellets to partially sort. Carefully tilt to transfer lighter or smaller pellets through the narrow connecting neck first. Build toward full separation one group at a time.

Bouci-Boula was likely a French puzzle import — the name has no clear English meaning but appears in French toy catalogs of the 1880s. Its elegant glass construction made it a desirable and attractive curiosity as well as a genuine puzzle.

A double glass bulb contains both red and white pellets mixed together. The puzzle is to separate them by color — all red in one bulb, all white in the other — without opening the glass. The solution requires carefully tilting all pellets to one side, shaking to group by color through size or density differences, then transferring small groups across the narrow neck.

svg.text: Source: Puzzles Old and New (1893) · Heritage Parlor
